question.text: As per SEBI regulations, a debenture trustee shall call for periodical status/performance reports from the issuer company within which of the following timelines?

answer.text: Within 15 days of the relevant board meeting or within 30 days of the respective quarter
Within 7 days of the relevant board meeting or within 45 days of the respective quarter, whichever is earlier
Within 30 days of the relevant board meeting
Within 60 days of the respective quarter

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 15(c) mandates that the debenture trustee shall call for periodical status/performance reports from the issuer company within 7 days of the relevant board meeting or within 45 days of the respective quarter, whichever is earlier. **The Trap:** The 'whichever is earlier' clause is critical -- some candidates may focus on only one timeline. **Cross-Exam Context:** Reporting timelines for debenture trustees are tested in NISM Series III-A and NISM Series IX exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set up a calendar system to track both board meeting dates and quarter-end dates to ensure timely requisition of reports.
